text: Email from @SHEIN_Official introducing a collection with the subtitle “build a conscious wardrobe”. Shein are a HUGE problem when it comes to environmental damage and exploitation of garment workers. This is greenwashing at its worst. Here’s my reply: #greenwashing #fastfashion https://t.co/pXCiXU12Iy
context_annotations: [{"domain": {"id": "47", "name": "Brand", "description": "Brands and Companies"}, "entity": {"id": "1415016995200765954", "name": "Shein"}}, {"domain": {"id": "131", "name": "Unified Twitter Taxonomy", "description": "A taxonomy of user interests. "}, "entity": {"id": "1415016995200765954", "name": "Shein"}}]
attachments.media:
[{"width": 750, "url": "https://pbs.twimg.com/media/FWgrt0sWYAM594E.jpg", "height": 1276, "alt_text": "Screenshot of an email to Shein. Text says: Hello,

Thanks for this email.

Unfortunately, I can’t see this as anything other than greenwashing. Adding some recycled polyester in a fraction of the clothing you offer is really not good enough. Your entire model is unsustainable, feeding a culture of disposable clothing that is out of style almost as soon as it’s delivered and so specific to microtrends that it isn't even able to be then passed on or resold. Your low prices prevent companies who use ethical sourcing of materials and working conditions from being able to compete and so you have a stranglehold on fashion. This claim to suddenly care about the various crises you have helped to build would be laughable if your company wasn't so damaging.", "type": "photo", "media_key": "3_1542530938613161987"}, {"width": 750, "url": "https://pbs.twimg.com/media/FWgrtztWIAE9Gxu.jpg", "height": 1357, "alt_text": "Continued email to Shein. Text says: 

Change all your materials to being recycled or sustainable and make your entire supply chain transparent to show you aren’t profiting from slavery and other exploitation. Make your business model circular, allowing customers to return their used clothes so you can reuse the materials - you of all clothing companies have the resources to do this. Then you can reasonably use ethical consumption as a marketing tool. 

To the person whose job it is to read this email - I know you don't have the personal power to make these changes. But please pass my message on as far as you can. We are at the stage where every decision made is a tiny shift towards the life or death of our planet. No action is irrelevant, and your choices do matter!

Best wishes

Carmella", "type": "photo", "media_key": "3_1542530938348904449"}]
